goal: Enable the guarded FIM route by default for DeepSeek-native single-shot and piped prompts when the prompt is an explicit safe single-file line-range edit, while preserving a CLI opt-out.
tests: DEEPSEEK_API_KEY=test cargo run --quiet --bin yoyo -- --deepseek-native --no-state --deepseek-fim-response '{"choices":[{"text":"//! default routed completion"}],"usage":{"prompt_tokens":5,"completion_tokens":2}}' --json --prompt 'complete src/deepseek.rs lines 2-3'
cargo run --quiet --bin yoyo -- eval fixtures validate --suite local-smoke
hidden_failure_mode: FIM routing exists but DeepSeek-native prompt mode still only uses it when callers know a hidden opt-in flag, so localized edit prompts default to the slower generic agent loop.
